UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account within the AgriPro Manager system, including administrative roles, farm managers, farm workers, and accountants who log in.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically corresponding to a Firebase Authentication user ID.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system, determining access permissions. (e.g., Admin, FarmManager, FarmWorker, Accountant).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

FarmWorker.title: FarmWorker
FarmWorker.description: Represents a farm worker whose labor is managed and tracked by the system, regardless of whether they have a login account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FarmWorker entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity if this worker has a login account. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FarmWorker)

name.description: The full name of the farm worker.

age.description: The age of the farm worker.

gender.description: The gender of the farm worker.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the farm worker.

assignedFieldIds.description: References to FarmField entities where this worker is currently assigned or works. (Relationship: FarmField N:N FarmWorker)

employmentType.description: The employment type of the worker (e.g., permanent, seasonal, daily wage).

wageRate.description: The rate at which the worker is paid.

wageRateUnit.description: The unit for the wage rate (e.g., 'per hour', 'per task').

workerPhotoUrl.description: URL to the worker's profile photo.

status.description: The current employment status of the worker (e.g., Active, Inactive).

managerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (Manager role) who is responsible for managing this worker. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FarmWorker)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the worker profile was created.

FarmField.title: FarmField
FarmField.description: Represents a specific agricultural field or plot within the farm.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FarmField entity.

name.description: The name or identifier of the field (e.g., 'North Field', 'Plot C').

sizeUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the field size (e.g., 'acres', 'hectares').

cropType.description: The primary crop type cultivated in this field (e.g., 'maize', 'rice', 'tomato').

season.description: The current or most recent planting season for this field (e.g., 'Spring 2023', 'Autumn 2024').

managerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (Manager role) who is responsible for this field. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FarmField)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the field record was created.

FarmTask.title: FarmTask
FarmTask.description: Represents a specific task assigned to farm workers within a field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FarmTask entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the FarmField where this task is to be performed. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N FarmTask)

cropType.description: The specific crop type related to this task, which may refine the field's general crop.

taskType.description: The type of farm activity (e.g., 'planting', 'weeding', 'harvesting', 'irrigation', 'fertilizer application').

description.description: A detailed description of the task, including instructions.

expectedOutput.description: The expected quantity or amount of work to be completed for this task.

expectedOutputUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the expected output (e.g., 'kg', 'acres', 'rows', 'units').

deadline.description: The target date and time for the task to be completed.

assignedWorkerIds.description: References to FarmWorker entities assigned to this task. (Relationship: FarmWorker N:N FarmTask)

status.description: The current status of the task (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

managerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (Manager role) who created and is managing this task. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FarmTask)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the task was created.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the task was marked as completed.

AttendanceRecord.title: AttendanceRecord
AttendanceRecord.description: Records the daily clock-in and clock-out times for a farm worker.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AttendanceRecord entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the FarmWorker whose attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: FarmWorker 1:N AttendanceRecord)

date.description: The date of the attendance record.

timeIn.description: The timestamp when the worker clocked in.

timeOut.description: The timestamp when the worker clocked out.

totalHoursWorked.description: The calculated total hours worked for the day.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the attendance record was created.

ProductivityEntry.title: ProductivityEntry
ProductivityEntry.description: Captures a single entry of output or work completed by a worker for a specific task and field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductivityEntry entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the FarmWorker who recorded this output. (Relationship: FarmWorker 1:N ProductivityEntry)

fieldId.description: Reference to the FarmField where the output was achieved. (Relationship: FarmField 1:N ProductivityEntry)

taskId.description: Reference to the FarmTask under which this output was achieved. (Relationship: FarmTask 1:N ProductivityEntry)

date.description: The date when the productivity was recorded.

outputQuantity.description: The quantity of output recorded (e.g., kg harvested, acres covered, number of rows planted).

outputUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the output quantity (e.g., 'kg', 'acres', 'rows', 'units').

hoursWorkedForEntry.description: The estimated or actual hours spent by the worker to achieve this specific output quantity for this entry.

notes.description: Additional notes or remarks for this productivity entry.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the productivity entry was created.

PayrollSummary.title: PayrollSummary
PayrollSummary.description: Contains the calculated payroll summary for a worker for a specific month.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PayrollSummary entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the FarmWorker for whom this payroll summary is generated. (Relationship: FarmWorker 1:N PayrollSummary)

month.description: The month number (1-12) for which the payroll is calculated.

year.description: The year for which the payroll is calculated.

totalHoursWorkedMonth.description: The total hours worked by the worker in this month, aggregated from attendance records.

totalTaskBasedPayment.description: The total payment calculated based on task output for the month, if applicable.

effectiveWageRate.description: The effective wage rate used for calculations in this payroll period, considering any adjustments.

effectiveWageRateUnit.description: The unit for the effective wage rate (e.g., 'per hour', 'per task').

totalPaymentDue.description: The total payment due to the worker for the entire month.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this payroll summary was generated.

definition.description: Stores authentication-linked user profiles. The `userId` is the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes the user's `role` (Admin, FarmManager, FarmWorker, Accountant). If the user is a logged-in FarmWorker, their corresponding `FarmWorker` document's ID (`farmWorkerId`) is assumed to be their `userId` for direct authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

definition.description: A collection for Role-Based Access Control. The existence of a document at `/system_roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants Admin privileges. Documents can be empty or contain a simple 'true' value. This supports 'existence over content' for global roles.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an Admin.

definition.description: A collection for Role-Based Access Control. The existence of a document at `/system_roles_accountant/$(request.auth.uid)` grants Accountant privileges. This supports efficient, existence-based role checks in security rules.

params.name: accountantId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an Accountant.

definition.description: Contains detailed profiles for all farm workers, whether they have a login account or not. Includes `managerId` (Firebase UID of the managing Farm Manager) for authorization independence. If the worker has a login, their Firebase `userId` (UID) is stored here, and for strict authorization independence in rules, `farmWorkerId` in the path matches this `userId`.

params.name: farmWorkerId
params.description: A unique identifier for the farm worker. For logged-in workers, this ID is their Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

definition.description: Stores information about specific farm fields. Includes `managerId` (Firebase UID) for the responsible Farm Manager, denormalized for authorization independence.

params.name: fieldId
params.description: A unique identifier for the farm field.

definition.description: Defines farm tasks. Includes `managerId` (Firebase UID) for the creator/manager (denormalized for authorization independence). The document includes `assignedWorkerIds` (array of `FarmWorker.id`s) as per schema, and to support authorization independence for logged-in workers, it will also contain denormalized `assignedWorkerUids` (array of Firebase UIDs) for assigned workers who have login accounts.

params.name: taskId
params.description: A unique identifier for the farm task.

definition.description: Stores individual attendance clock-in/out records, scoped to a specific farm worker. The `workerId` field in the document must match `farmWorkerId` from the path. For logged-in workers, `farmWorkerId` is their Firebase UID, enabling direct self-access in security rules.

params.name: farmWorkerId
params.description: The unique ID of the farm worker to whom this attendance record belongs. For logged-in workers, this is their Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

params.name: recordId
params.description: A unique identifier for the attendance record.

definition.description: Stores individual productivity entries, scoped to a specific farm worker. The `workerId` field in the document must match `farmWorkerId` from the path. Includes denormalized `fieldId` and `taskId` for context. For logged-in workers, `farmWorkerId` is their Firebase UID, enabling direct self-access in security rules.

params.name: farmWorkerId
params.description: The unique ID of the farm worker to whom this productivity entry belongs. For logged-in workers, this is their Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

params.name: entryId
params.description: A unique identifier for the productivity entry.

definition.description: Stores monthly payroll summaries, scoped to a specific farm worker. The `workerId` field in the document must match `farmWorkerId` from the path. For logged-in workers, `farmWorkerId` is their Firebase UID, enabling direct self-access in security rules.

params.name: farmWorkerId
params.description: The unique ID of the farm worker to whom this payroll summary belongs. For logged-in workers, this is their Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

params.name: summaryId
params.description: A unique identifier for the payroll summary.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs, by leveraging structural segregation and strategic denormalization. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Denormalized Manager IDs:** For `FarmWorker`, `FarmField`, and `FarmTask` entities, the `managerId` (Firebase UID of the managing Farm Manager) is explicitly stored within each document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent documents or external collections to determine if a manager has permission to access or modify a resource. Security rules can directly check `resource.data.managerId == request.auth.uid`, ensuring atomic and efficient authorization checks.
2.  **Denormalized Worker UIDs for Tasks:** The `FarmTask` entity will include an additional denormalized field, `assignedWorkerUids` (an array of Firebase UIDs of assigned workers with login accounts). This allows a logged-in worker to query and verify access to their assigned tasks by checking `request.auth.uid in resource.data.assignedWorkerUids` without relying on `get()` calls or complex lookups across collections. 
3.  **Self-Owned Data for Workers:** For worker-specific data (Attendance, Productivity, Payroll) stored as subcollections under `/farm_workers/{farmWorkerId}`, the `farmWorkerId` in the path is assumed to be the Firebase UID (`request.auth.uid`) for logged-in workers. This crucial design choice enables direct path-based authorization for individual workers, allowing security rules to simply verify `farmWorkerId == request.auth.uid`. For workers without a login, `farmWorkerId` will be a system-generated ID, and their data will be managed by their assigned managers or admins. This approach ensures atomic self-access without any `get()` calls for path resolution.

**QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters):**
1.  **Structural Segregation for Roles:** Dedicated collections like `/system_roles_admin/{adminId}` and `/system_roles_accountant/{accountantId}` provide clear, existence-based authorization for top-level roles. The `role` field in `/user_profiles/{userId}` defines other roles, allowing rules to check `request.auth.role == 'Admin'` or `request.auth.role == 'FarmManager'` after fetching the user's profile (which is implicitly available as `request.auth.token.role`). 
2.  **Manager-Scoped Data:** `FarmWorker`, `FarmField`, and `FarmTask` documents include `managerId`. This allows managers to issue queries like `db.collection('farm_workers').where('managerId', '==', currentUser.uid)` which are directly supported by security rules, ensuring that `list` operations only return authorized data and do not rely on rules to filter results after retrieval.
3.  **Worker-Scoped Data:** Subcollections like `/farm_workers/{farmWorkerId}/attendance_records` ensure that data pertaining to a specific worker is logically grouped. For logged-in workers, if `farmWorkerId` is their Firebase UID, queries are simple and rules can ensure self-access. Otherwise, the data is accessible by managers (via `managerId` on the parent `farm_workers` document) and admins.

This structure prioritizes flat collections for commonly accessed entities (workers, fields, tasks) that require manager-level access, and hierarchical subcollections for data strictly owned by or pertaining to a specific worker, ensuring both scalability and fine-grained access control with minimal rule complexity.
